texto: Before, you are wise; after, you are wise. In between you are otherwise
explicacao: Citação de David Zindell sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “Before, you are wise; after, you are wise. In between you are otherwise” articula sabedoria, hindsight, before em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como sabedoria.

texto: The number two, he thought, was an ominous number. Two is a reflection or duplication of one, the most perfect of the natural numbers. Two is all echo and counterpoise; two is the beginning of multiplicity, the way the universal oneness differentiates itself and breaks apart into strings and quarks and photons, all the separate and component pieces of life. Two is a symbol of becoming as opposed to pure being...

texto: 1620Each quantum event, each of the trillions of times reality’s particles interact with each other every instant, is like a note that rings and resonates throughout the great bell of creation. And the sound of the ringing propagates instantaneously, everywhere at once, interconnecting all things. This is a truth of our universe. It is a mystical truth, that reality at its deepest level is an undivided wholeness.~ David Zindell
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David Zindell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “1620Each quantum event, each of the trillions of times reality’s particles interact with each oth…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, a-requiem-for-homo-sapiens, david-zindell pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de david-zindell e neverness.
